top_findings.title: Test binary fails to compile — no tests execute
top_findings.evidence: go test ./... => FAIL [build failed]; analyzer_test.go:454,518,547,574,600,... call MCP handlers with the pre-migration signature (handler(nil) / handler(map[string]interface{}{...}) and type-assert *mcp.CallToolResult as an interface). 10 handler call-sites are broken; the whole package's tests are unbuildable.
top_findings.suggestion: Rewrite the 10 MCP-handler tests to call handler(context.Background(), mcp.CallToolRequest{Arguments: map[string]any{...}}) and read *mcp.CallToolResult (result.Content[0].(mcp.TextContent).Text), matching server.go's ToolHandlerFunc.

top_findings.title: Stored test_coverage=0.815 contradicts a build-failed test suite
top_findings.evidence: scores.json reports {"test_coverage": 0.815}, but `go test ./...` returns 'FAIL [build failed]' — the test binary does not compile, so 0 tests run. The stored mechanical score overstates verification; the true test gate value is 0.
top_findings.suggestion: Treat this run as failing the test gate. Investigate why retort's Go test_coverage scorer produced 0.815 for a package whose test binary fails to build (it should return 0).

top_findings.title: Head-to-head W/L/D record is never computed (dead code)
top_findings.evidence: analyzer.go:272 collectH2H returns the match list but the win/loss/draw counting is dead: h2hDummy := &HeadToHead{...} is assigned then discarded (analyzer.go:295) and branches only do `_ = homeScore` (analyzer.go:297-306). HeadToHead.Team1Wins/Team2Wins/Draws/TotalMatches stay 0. The get_head_to_head tool therefore returns matches with an all-zero W/L/D summary.
top_findings.suggestion: Increment h2h.Team1Wins/Team2Wins/Draws and TotalMatches inside collectH2H based on the score comparison, and return them on the HeadToHead struct.
